title: Induction of root symbioses in common bean
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"71ejq","text":"A hallmark of legume plants is their ability to establish mutualistic symbioses with Rhizobium bacteria and arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (Rhizophagus irregularis). These symbionts help in providing nutritional benefits to the host plants. Herein, we provide a protocol to inoculate the common bean for the induction of symbiont colonization. This protocol has been routinely used in our laboratory with high reproducibility.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":85,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":138,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: About one gram of Rhizophagus irregularis spores (Symplanta®, Germany) were dissolved in 10 ml sterile water.
Using hemocytometer adjust the spore count to 1000 spores/ml by dilution with sterile water.
To induce mycorrhizal colonization, inoculate 1 ml of culture (prepared from step 1 & 2) at the root zone of 5 day old common bean seedlings maintained in pots containing sterile vermiculite.
Maintain the inoculated plants in growth chambers with a 16-h photoperiod and 65% relative humidity at 28 °C.
Irrigate the plants regularly with B&D solution to promote mycorrhizal colonization.
In wild type plants mature arbuscules will be seen form 14 days post inoculation.

For induction of root nodules, inoculate Rhizobium tropici strain CIAT899 (this species is compatible to common bean) 100 ml of PY medium (0.5 g peptone, 0.3 g yeast extract) supplemented with 7 mM ml –1 CaCl 2 and 20 mg ml –1 nalidixic acid and incubate at 30 °C for 24 h with shaking at 200 rpm. NOTE: Any specific antibiotics could be used in case of transgenic Rhizobium .
Pellet the cells in a centrifuge for 3 min at 5000 rpm at room temperature and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in 10 mM MgSO 4 or sterile water.
Adjust the OD of rhizobia cells to 0.05 at OD 600 by dilution with 10 mM MgSO 4 or sterile water.
To induce nodulation, inoculate 1 ml of culture (prepared from step 3 & 4) at the root zone of 5 day old common bean seedlings maintained in pots containing sterile vermiculite.
Maintain the inoculated plants in growth chambers with a 16-h photoperiod and 65% relative humidity at 28 °C.
Irrigate the plants regularly with B&D solution to promote nodulation.
In wild type plants the mature nodules will be seen form 14 days post inoculation (dpi) to 21 dpi.
creator: Manoj-Kumar Arthikala
